guideline_references: Vascular guidance on acute limb ischaemia
RACGP guidance on peripheral neuropathy work-up
intentional_test_features: Red herrings: severe burning limb pain with orthostatic dizziness mimics acute limb ischaemia, DVT and hypotensive shock.
Important-not-to-miss: acute limb ischaemia and ascending motor weakness (GBS) cannot be examined remotely; high fall risk from dysautonomia.
Anchoring: 'known small fibre neuropathy' should not close off a vascular limb emergency or an evolving motor neuropathy.
Commission trap: amitriptyline started stat over chat is reprojected to a GP-initiated adjunct; supportive measures are advised.
Telehealth limits: neurological/vascular examination and pulses cannot be assessed over chat.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
